title: leptinella traillii
intro: Leptinella traillii, also known as small cudweed, is a small, creeping herb native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its delicate, rounded leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. Leptinella traillii is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Leptinella traillii include infusions, teas, and topical creams, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or applying them directly to the skin.
